rulings.Case Title: HOWARD I. MAIBACH VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, A
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Application For Leave To File Special Motion To Strike Pursuant To Ccp Section 425.16
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, January 13, 2025, Line 1, RESPONDENT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's Amended Notice Of Application For Leave To File Special Motion To Strike Pursuant To CCP Section 425.16. Off calendar. The moving party failed to provide courtesy copies in compliance with SF Local Rule 2.7B. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: BANK OF AMERICA, N.A. VS. KATHLEEN NICOLE SOLANO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Motion To Request To Set Aside Conditional Settlement To Enforce Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, January 13, 2025, Line 2, PLAINTIFF BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.'s Motion To Request To Set Aside Conditional Settlement To Enforce Settlement. Plaintiff Bank of America, N.A.'s unopposed motion to set aside conditional settlement to enforce settlement, which in effect is a motion for entry of judgment pursuant to the parties' conditional settlement, is GRANTED. Judgment shall be entered and, pursuant to the parties' agreement, Plaintiff shall recover $3,964.38 in principal, and $318.50 in costs, for a total of $5.282.88. The court notes going forward, counsel must file a proposed order on the motion and, separately, a proposed judgment conforming to the requirements in the CCP and California Rules of Court. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: LILY MAI VS. FCS BARBER - BUFFA L.L.C., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Final Approval Of Class Action And Paga Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, January 13, 2025, Line 4, 2- PLAINTIFF LILY MAI AN INDIVIDUAL, ON BEHALF OF HERSELF AND ALL OTHERS SIMILARLY SITUATED's Motion For Final Approval Of Class Action And PAGA Settlement. Plaintiff Lily Mai's Motion For Final Approval Of Class Action And PAGA Settlement as set out in the proposed order and proposed judgment is GRANTED, except that the court will set a hearing (not a non-appearance case review) re disposition of the settlement proceeds for August 18, 2025, at 9:00 am in this department and order Plaintiff's counsel to file a declaration re same no later than August 7, 2025, with courtesy copy provided within 24 hours of filing.
